items.briefing:
A large-scale credential theft campaign has compromised over 30,000 Fortinet network devices globally, meaning stolen login details could be used to access organisational systems without triggering obvious alarms. Separately, INC ransomware continues to cause damage by exploiting basic security gaps — no sophisticated techniques required. Australian organisations should treat both as a reminder that weak or reused passwords remain a serious vulnerability.

There is also growing local discussion around organisations having a "digital duty of care" when using AI tools, with accountability for AI-related harms coming under increasing scrutiny. **Practical tip:** Audit staff accounts today and remove any that are shared, unused, or haven't had their passwords changed recently.

featured.title: Mackay Sugar cyber attack flagged as broader risk to Australia’s food supply chain
featured.quote: Major Australian food exporter hit by cyber attack, exposing supply chain vulnerability.
